description_long: This object is a vintage box camera, likely made of black plastic with a textured, matte finish. The front features a prominent circular lens assembly with the word 'DRUH' and a ridged focus ring, as well as a small rectangular viewfinder above the lens. The top of the camera has a round winding knob and a smaller cylindrical post, possibly for film advance or shutter release. The back is flat with two circular windows for viewing film frame numbers and embossed markings. The sides are ribbed for grip, with a visible button or latch on each side. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, typical of mid-20th-century consumer cameras.
description: A vintage black plastic box camera with a prominent lens, winding knob, and ribbed sides for grip.
description_view_0: The back of the camera, showing two circular film windows and embossed markings.
description_view_1: The left side, highlighting the ribbed grip and a round button or latch.
description_view_2: The front, featuring the large lens assembly, viewfinder, and brand markings.
description_view_3: The right side, showing the ribbed grip and the winding knob on top.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
